Notice And Motion To Be Relieved From Waiver Of Objections To Written Discovery
Off Calendar Sep-01-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEFFREY L. JACOBS OF THE JACOBS LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-916-569-1600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. CHANTEL E. LAFRADES OF THE MURCHISON & CUMMING LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-524-4308 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MISTI JOHNSON'S Motion To Be Relieved From Waiver Of Objections To Written Discovery is GRANTED. Unless the error is based on mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect, a party who fails to timely respond to requests for Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Request for Production of Document waives "any objection" to these requests, "including one based on privilege" or the protection of attorney work product. (Code Civ. Pro. 2030.290-2031.300.) The purpose of similar mandatory relief provisions is to alleviate the hardship on parties who suffer due to an inexcusable failure to act by their attorneys. (Zamora v. Clayborn Contracting Group, Inc. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249, 257.) It is, therefore, within this Court's discretion to grant Plaintiff Johnson's motion on the grounds that her error in timely filing discovery responses was due to the mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect by prior counsel. Plaintiff Johnson substituted counsel after her former counsel failed to provide timely discovery responses. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
